name: A Fala (Xalimego)
notes: A Fala (Xalimego/Fala de Xálima). Galician-Portuguese linguistic island in Cáceres, Extremadura. Three local varieties: Valverdeiru (Valverde del Fresno), Lagarteiro (Eljas), Mañegu (San Martín de Trevejo). ~5,000–10,000 speakers. Preserves medieval Galician-Portuguese features with Leonese/Extremaduran influence (F- → [h] aspiration in some words). Betacism. Nasal vowels. Palatal [ʎ]. ISO 639-3: fax. SIGNAL FEATURE — FOUR-WAY MEDIEVAL SIBILANT SYSTEM (Grupo Xálima tesoro p.38, table p.53): A Fala preserves the full voiced/voiceless sibilant contrast otherwise kept only by Portuguese — intervocalic ⟨s⟩ = voiced [z] (casa) vs ⟨ss⟩ = [s] (nossa); ⟨x⟩ = voiceless [ʃ] (queixu) vs ⟨j⟩ / ⟨g⟩ before e,i = voiced [ʒ] (queiju, ovelha) — hence ⟨j⟩ and ⟨g⟩(e,i) are mapped to [ʒ], NOT the voiceless [ʃ] of Castilian jota; plus interdental /θ/ from ⟨c⟩(e,i)/⟨ç⟩ (cincu, caça, poçu). So NO seseo and NO ceceo, and voicing is phonemic. SUB-VARIETY SPLIT (documented, not phoneme-split): medieval z (⟨z⟩ here defaulted to [θ]) is realised as a voiced interdental [ð̞] in lagarteiru/mañegu but merges to plain [θ] in valverdeiru (tesoro pp.54-55) — this spec keeps the valverdeiru [θ] default. Other per-variety/overlay traits out of grapheme->IPA scope: final-vowel metaphony -e>-i/-o>-u, implosive -r/-l -> [l], intervocalic -d- loss, final -z drop, preterite cantaram (valverdeiru) vs cantórim (lagarteiru/mañegu).

sources.author: Grupo de Estudo d'A Fala (Grupo Xálima)
sources.title: A Fala de Xálima: valverdeiru, lagarteiru, manhegu. Un tesoro luso-extremeño
sources.notes: Read directly (papers/iberian/fala_xalima_valverdeiru_lagarteiru_manhegu_tesoro.pdf). p.37-39 Galician-Portuguese diagnostics (no Ĕ/Ŏ diphthongisation, F- preserved vs Sierra-de-Gata Astur-Leonese /x-/, -LL-/-NN- degemination, -N- loss, nasal vowels); p.38 & table p.53 the four-way sibilant system casa/z/ vs nossa/s/, queixu/ʃ/ vs queiju/ʒ/, interdental /θ/ — the basis for adding /ʒ/ and confirming the voiced intervocalic /z/; p.54-55 valverdeiru z→[θ] vs lagarteiru/mañegu [ð̞]. Year approximate.

sources.author: Carrasco González, J.M.
sources.title: A Fala (do Val de Xálima)
sources.notes: Read (papers/iberian/carrasco_fala_ehumanista_2023.pdf). Modern descriptive synthesis; corroborates the Galician-Portuguese classification and the three sub-varieties.

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
